messages.content: Please accomplish this task: Navigate to https://www.google.com/maps and wait for the page to fully load, including any dynamic elements. If a cookie consent banner or a sign-in prompt appears, dismiss it or accept only the necessary cookies to avoid blocking the interface. In the search box located at the top-left corner (an input field with placeholder text like "Search Google Maps"), type "coffee shops in London" and press Enter or click the magnifying glass search button. Wait for the results list to load on the left sidebar — look for a scrollable container filled with place cards, each containing a business name and details. Once the result cards are rendered (you can verify by the presence of at least 5 visible cards), scrape the following for the very first 5 results only: the business name (usually found in an element like an h2 or a div with a class containing "fontHeadlineSmall" or inside an aria-label) and the average rating (typically a span with an aria-label like "XX stars" or a numeric value next to gold stars). Record these name-rating pairs. Do not scroll or click pagination; collect only the top 5 as they appear initially. The task is successful once you have extracted exactly 5 names and their corresponding ratings, each being a non-empty string. Be prepared for lazy-loaded images and ratings that might take a moment to appear, and note that some listings might have no rating — in that case, record "N/A" for that field. Handle any "Search this area" prompts by ignoring them or clicking “Dismiss” if they obstruct the results.

response.content: The page has loaded. I can see the search box. No cookie banner or sign-in prompt is blocking the interface. Let me type the search query.
